title: Line 12905 – Taxable first home savings account (FHSA) income - Canada.ca
content:
## Line 12905 – Taxable first home savings account (FHSA) income
Taxable FHSA income includes amounts that must be reported as income for the year, such as:
- taxable withdrawals from your FHSAs
- amounts you are required to include in income because property remained in your FHSA after it ceased to be an FHSA

## Completing your tax return
Enter the total of the following amounts on line 12905 of your return:
- Box 22 of all T4FHSA slips
- Box 26 of your T4FHSA slip if the amount you were deemed to have received when the FHSA ceased relates to your own FHSA
Do not include qualifying withdrawals used to purchase a qualifying first home. These withdrawals are not taxable.
For more information about FHSAs, including contributions, withdrawals, and when an FHSA ceases to be an FHSA, see First Home Savings Account (FHSA) .

## Forms and publications
- T4FHSA, First Home Savings Account Statement
- Line 12906 – Taxable FHSA income – other
- Line 20805 – FHSA deduction
- Line 23200 – Other deductions

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
